title: vicia glauca subsp. giennensis
intro: Vicia glauca subsp. giennensis, also known as blue vetch, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy among local populations. Blue vetch can be prepared as a decoction or infusion to be used in herbal medicine, and its aerial parts are often used to make teas or infusions for its medicinal properties.
